html:
[1207]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先是 - Temporal marker meaning 'previously' or 'at first', setting the scene for the narrative.
  • 不茹荤酒 - Means 'does not eat meat or drink alcohol', indicating a vegetarian and abstemious lifestyle.
  • 治洁室 - Means 'prepare a clean room', describing the setting for religious practice.
  • 悬观音像 - Means 'hang a statue of Guanyin', referring to the Bodhisattva of Compassion.
  • 以无子，日日焚祷其中 - Means 'because she had no children, she burned incense and prayed there daily', indicating a religious practice related to fertility.
  • 辄示梦，教人趋避 - Means 'often shows dreams, teaching people to avoid or seek', suggesting that the statue has supernatural powers.
  • 家中事皆取决焉 - Means 'all household matters are decided by it', indicating the statue's influence on family decisions.
  • 有疾綦笃 - Means 'suffered from a very serious illness', describing the severity of the illness.
  • 移榻其中 - Means 'move her bed there', indicating that the sick person is moved to the room with the statue.
  • 别设锦裀于内室而扃其户 - Means 'set up a silk mat in the inner room and lock the door', suggesting a private space for the sick person.
  • 若有所伺 - Means 'as if waiting for something', indicating a sense of anticipation.
  • 王以为惑 - Means 'the king thought it was foolish', expressing skepticism.
  • 以其疾势昏瞀 - Means 'because of her severe illness and confusion', explaining why the king did not interfere.
  • 卧病二年，恶嚣，常屏人独寝 - Means 'lay ill for two years, hating noise, often staying alone in bed', describing the long-term illness and isolation.
  • 潜听之似与人语，启门视之又寂然 - Means 'listening secretly, it seemed like she was talking to someone, but opening the door, it was silent again', describing a mysterious phenomenon.
  • 病中他无所虑，有女十四岁，惟日催治装遣嫁 - Means 'in her illness, she had no other concerns, only her fourteen-year-old daughter, urging her daily to prepare for her marriage', indicating the daughter's role in the narrative.
  • 既醮 - Means 'after the wedding ceremony', marking a significant event in the narrative.
  • 执手曰 - Means 'took her hand and said', describing a poignant moment.
  • 今诀矣 - Means 'now we part', indicating a farewell.
  • 初病时，菩萨告我，命当速死 - Means 'at the beginning of my illness, the Bodhisattva told me, my fate is to die soon', describing a divine message.
  • 念不了者，幼女未嫁 - Means 'thinking of what remains undone, my young daughter is unmarried', indicating a concern for the daughter's future.
  • 因赐少药，俾延息以待 - Means 'so she gave me a little medicine, to prolong my life until then', describing a divine intervention.
  • 去岁，菩萨将回南海 - Means 'last year, the Bodhisattva was returning to the South Sea', indicating a change in the statue's status.
  • 留案前侍女小梅，为妾服役 - Means 'left a maid named Xiaomei to serve me', describing a new addition to the household.
  • 今将死，薄命人又无所出 - Means 'now that I am dying, this unfortunate person has no children', expressing a sense of despair.
  • 保儿，专所怜爱，恐娶悍怒之妇，令其子母失所 - Means 'Ba'er, whom I especially loved, I fear he will marry a harsh and angry woman, causing him and his mother to suffer', expressing concern for the son's future.
  • 小梅姿容秀美，又温淑，即以为继室可也 - Means 'Xiaomei is beautiful and gentle, so she can be his second wife', suggesting a solution to the problem.
  • 王以其言荒唐 - Means 'the king thought her words were absurd', expressing skepticism.
  • 卿素敬者神，今出此言，不已亵乎 - Means 'you always respect the gods, now to say such things, isn't that disrespectful?', expressing a moral judgment.
  • 答云 - Means 'she replied', indicating a response.
  • 小梅事我年余，相忘形骸，我已婉求之矣 - Means 'Xiaomei has served me for over a year, we have forgotten our physical forms, I have already gently asked her', describing the relationship between the sick woman and Xiaomei.
  • 问：“小梅何处？”曰：“室中非耶？” - Means 'asked, 'Where is Xiaomei?' she replied, 'Isn't she in the room?'', indicating the location of Xiaomei.
  • 方欲再诘，闭目已逝 - Means 'just as he was about to ask again, she closed her eyes and passed away', marking the end of the narrative.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王妻 - Refers to the king's wife, a central figure in the narrative.
  • 贤而好佛 - Describes the queen as virtuous and devoted to Buddhism.
  • 观音像 - Refers to the statue of Guanyin, a significant religious icon in Chinese Buddhism.
  • 南海 - Refers to the South Sea, a place associated with the Bodhisattva Guanyin in Buddhist tradition.
  • 醮 - Refers to the wedding ceremony, a significant cultural event.
  • 保儿 - Refers to the king's son, a key figure in the narrative.
  • 小梅 - Refers to the maid, a new character introduced in the narrative.

Proper Nouns

  • 王 - Refers to the king, a central figure in the narrative.
  • 王妻 - Refers to the king's wife, a central figure in the narrative.
  • 观音 - Refers to the Bodhisattva Guanyin, a significant religious figure.
  • 南海 - Refers to the South Sea, a place associated with the Bodhisattva Guanyin in Buddhist tradition.
  • 保儿 - Refers to the king's son, a key figure in the narrative.
  • 小梅 - Refers to the maid, a new character introduced in the narrative.

[1208]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 夜守灵帏 - To guard or watch over a spirit tent, which is a euphemism for guarding a deceased person's body.
  • 闻室中隐隐啜泣 - Faint sobbing, describing a soft and subdued sound.
  • 疑为鬼 - To suspect it is a ghost.
  • 唤诸婢妾启钥视之 - To call the maidservants and unlock to look inside.
  • 二八丽者缞服在室 - A beautiful woman of sixteen (eight times two) in mourning attire in the room.
  • 众以为神，共罗拜之 - The people thought she was a deity and all prostrated themselves in worship.
  • 女敛涕扶掖 - To wipe away tears and support or assist.
  • 如果亡室之言非妄 - If the words of the deceased wife are not false.
  • 请即上堂，受儿女朝谒 - Please come upstairs to receive the greetings of the children.
  • 如其不可，仆亦不敢妄想，以取罪过 - If it is not possible, I would also not dare to hope, as it would bring guilt.
  • 女靦然出，竟登北堂 - She shyly appeared and finally ascended to the north hall.
  • 王使婢为设坐南向 - To have the maidservants set up a seat facing south.
  • 王先拜，女亦答拜 - The king bowed first, and the woman returned the bow.
  • 下而长幼卑贱，以次伏叩 - Descending in order of age and status, prostrating themselves in turn.
  • 女庄容坐受，惟妾至则挽之 - Sitting with a solemn expression and accepting, only holding the hands of the concubines.
  • 自夫人卧病，婢惰奴偷，家久替 - The lady was ill, the maidservants were lazy, the servants were thieving, and the household had fallen into disarray.
  • 众参已，肃肃列侍 - The people had finished paying respects and stood in order, respectfully.
  • 我感夫人盛意，羁留人间，又以大事相委 - I am grateful for the lady's great kindness, detained in the world, and entrusted with important matters.
  • 汝辈宜各洗心，为主效力，从前愆尤，悉不计较 - You should cleanse your hearts, serve the master, and not hold past faults against anyone.
  • 不然，莫谓室无人也 - Otherwise, do not think the room is empty.
  • 真如悬观音图像，时被微风吹动 - Truly like a hanging image of Guanyin, occasionally moved by a gentle breeze.
  • 闻言悚惕，哄然并诺 - Startled upon hearing it, agreeing in unison.
  • 女乃排拨丧务，一切井井 - Arranging funeral affairs, everything in order.
  • 由是大小无敢懈者 - No one, big or small, dared to slack off.
  • 女终日经纪内外 - Managing both inside and outside affairs.
  • 王将有作，亦禀白而行 - About to do something, reporting and proceeding.
  • 然虽一夕数见，并不交一私语 - Meeting several times a night, without exchanging a single private word.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王 - Refers to a king or prince, indicating a high-ranking noble.
  • 灵帏 - A tent used for mourning rituals, symbolizing the solemnity of the occasion.
  • 二八丽者 - A term often used to describe a young and beautiful woman, typically sixteen years old.
  • 缞服 - Mourning attire, indicating the woman's status as a mourner.
  • 罗拜 - A form of prostration used in worship or respect.
  • 亡室之言 - The words of the deceased wife, suggesting a spiritual or supernatural element.
  • 北堂 - The northern hall, often associated with the most honored position in a household.
  • 观音 - Guanyin, the bodhisattva of compassion, often depicted in art.
  • 丧务 - Funeral affairs, indicating the management of a death-related event.
  • 井井 - An idiom meaning everything is in order and well-managed.
  • 经纪内外 - Managing both internal and external affairs, indicating comprehensive responsibility.
  • 禀白而行 - Reporting and proceeding, indicating a formal and respectful process.
  • 私语 - Private conversation, suggesting a lack of intimacy or personal connection.

Proper Nouns

  • 王 - The king or prince.
  • 灵帏 - The spirit tent or mourning tent.
  • 二八丽者 - A beautiful woman of sixteen years old.
  • 缞服 - Mourning attire.
  • 北堂 - The northern hall.
  • 观音 - Guanyin, the bodhisattva of compassion.
  • 丧务 - Funeral affairs.
  • 井井 - An idiom meaning everything is in order.
  • 经纪内外 - Managing both inside and outside affairs.

[1209]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 既殡 - Refers to after the funeral rites have been performed.
  • 王欲申前约 - Means 'the king wanted to fulfill the previous agreement.'
  • 不敢径告 - Means 'did not dare to inform directly.'
  • 嘱妾微示意 - Means 'entrusted me to give a subtle hint.'
  • 妾受夫人谆嘱 - Means 'I received the lady's earnest instructions.'
  • 义不容辞 - Means 'cannot avoid duty.'
  • 但匹配大礼 - Means 'but the grand ceremony of marriage.'
  • 不得草草 - Means 'cannot be done hastily.'
  • 年伯黄先生位尊德重 - Means 'Uncle Huang, the respected and virtuous scholar.'
  • 求使主秦晋之盟 - Means 'asked him to oversee the alliance between Qin and Jin.'
  • 惟命是听 - Means 'to follow orders.'
  • 沂水黄太仆致仕闲居 - Means 'Mr. Huang, the retired Grand Steward from Yishui, who was retired and living in leisure.'
  • 于王为父执 - Means 'who was a senior official to the king.'
  • 往来最善 - Means 'had the closest relationship.'
  • 即亲诣 - Means 'immediately went in person.'
  • 以实告 - Means 'told him the truth.'
  • 黄奇之 - Means 'Mr. Huang was surprised.'
  • 即与同来 - Means 'immediately came along with him.'
  • 女闻，即出展拜 - Means 'the girl heard and immediately came out to pay respects.'
  • 黄一见，惊为天人 - Means 'Mr. Huang, upon seeing her, was amazed by her beauty.'
  • 逊谢不敢当礼 - Means 'modestly declined to accept the formalities.'
  • 既而助妆优厚 - Means 'then provided generous assistance with the dowry.'
  • 成礼乃去 - Means 'after the ceremony, he left.'
  • 女馈遗枕履，若奉舅姑 - Means 'the girl gave gifts of pillows and slippers, as if presenting them to her in-laws.'
  • 由此交益亲 - Means 'thus, their relationship became even closer.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 秦晋之盟 - Refers to the alliance between the states of Qin and Jin, symbolizing a prestigious and significant union.
  • 致仕 - Refers to retirement from official duties.
  • 父执 - Refers to a senior official or mentor.
  • 展拜 - Refers to paying respects formally.
  • 天人 - Refers to someone of extraordinary beauty and virtue, often used to describe a divine or near-divine figure.
  • 助妆 - Refers to providing financial or material assistance for the bride's dowry.
  • 舅姑 - Refers to the husband's parents, specifically the father-in-law and mother-in-law.

Proper Nouns

  • 王 - Refers to the king.
  • 夫人 - Refers to the lady or noblewoman.
  • 妾 - Refers to the speaker, a woman in a lower social position.
  • 黄先生 - Refers to Mr. Huang, a respected scholar.
  • 沂水黄太仆 - Refers to Mr. Huang, the retired Grand Steward from Yishui.
  • 女 - Refers to the girl or young woman.

[1210]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 合卺后 - An ancient Chinese wedding ritual where the bride and groom share a cup of wine.
  • 王终以神故，亵中带肃 - Describes the king maintaining a balance between intimacy and solemnity.
  • 研诘菩萨起居 - "研诘" means to inquire or investigate, and "菩萨起居" refers to the daily activities or habits of a bodhisattva.
  • 君亦太愚，焉有正直之神，而下婚尘世者？ - A rhetorical question expressing disbelief that a righteous deity would marry into the mundane world.
  • 女御下常宽，非笑不语 - The woman does not speak unless she is laughing.
  • 岂尔辈尚以我为神耶？我何神哉！ - A rhetorical question asking if the people still consider her a deity, followed by a self-deprecating statement.
  • 实为夫人姨妹，少相交好；姊病见思，阴使南村王姥招我来。 - Explains that she is actually the sister-in-law's aunt and was brought there because her sister was ill.
  • 第以日近姊夫，有男女之嫌，故托为神道，闭内室中 - Indicates that she was placed in a room as a deity to avoid any impropriety due to her proximity to the sister's husband.
  • 众犹不信 - The people still did not believe her explanation.
  • 日侍边旁，见其举动，不少异于常人 - Describes how the people observed her actions and found them no different from those of an ordinary person.
  • 浮言渐息 - The rumors or gossip gradually subsided.
  • 顽奴钝婢，王素挞楚所不能化者 - Refers to the servants who were usually difficult to discipline.
  • 女一言无不乐于奉命 - Indicates that the woman's words were always followed with joy.
  • 并不自知。实非畏之；但睹其貌，则心自柔，故不忍拂其意耳 - Explains that the servants did not fear her but were softened by her appearance and thus did not want to go against her wishes.
  • 以此百废具举 - Means that with her presence, many neglected tasks were taken care of.
  • 数年中，田地连阡，仓禀万石矣 - Describes the economic prosperity that resulted from her presence over several years.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 合卺 - An ancient Chinese wedding ritual where the bride and groom share a cup of wine.
  • 菩萨 - A bodhisattva, an enlightened being in Buddhism.
  • 姨妹 - Aunt, specifically the sister of one's spouse's mother.
  • 姊病见思 - The illness of the sister, which led to the aunt being brought to the household.
  • 阴使南村王姥招我来 - The use of a go-between (王姥) to bring the aunt to the household.
  • 男女之嫌 - Refers to the avoidance of improper relationships between men and women.

Proper Nouns

  • 王 - The king or lord of the household.
  • 女御 - The woman, who is the aunt of the sister-in-law.
  • 夫人 - The sister-in-law.
  • 姊 - The sister of the sister-in-law.
  • 南村王姥 - The go-between who brought the aunt to the household.

[1211]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又數年 - Means 'several years later,' indicating a passage of time.
  • 妾產一女 - Means 'I gave birth to a daughter,' with '妾' referring to the speaker as a humble woman.
  • 女生一子 - Means 'the daughter gave birth to a son,' indicating a generational shift.
  • 左臂有朱点 - Means 'a red dot on the left arm,' describing a physical mark.
  • 因字小红 - Means 'therefore, she was named Xiao Hong,' where '字' refers to a courtesy name.
  • 弥月 - Means 'after a month,' referring to the period following childbirth.
  • 女使王盛筵招黄 - Means 'Lady Wang held a grand banquet to invite Huang,' where '女使' refers to a female attendant or servant.
  • 贺仪丰渥 - Means 'the gifts were generous and abundant,' describing the hospitality.
  • 但辞以耄，不能远涉 - Means 'but Huang declined, citing his old age and inability to travel far,' where '耄' refers to old age.
  • 遣两媪强邀之 - Means 'sent two elderly women to strongly invite him,' where '媪' refers to elderly women.
  • 袒其左臂 - Means 'she exposed her left arm,' indicating a gesture to show the red dot.
  • 命名之意 - Means 'the meaning behind the name,' referring to the reason for the name.
  • 又再三问其吉凶 - Means 'she repeatedly asked about the auspiciousness,' indicating a concern for fortune.
  • 此喜红也 - Means 'this is auspicious red,' interpreting the red dot as a sign of good luck.
  • 可增一字，名喜红 - Means 'an additional character can be added, naming her Xi Hong,' suggesting a name change.
  • 鼓乐充庭，贵戚如市 - Means 'music filled the courtyard, and nobles gathered like a market,' describing a lively and festive scene.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 妾 - Refers to a humble woman, often used by women to describe themselves in a modest manner.
  • 字 - Refers to a courtesy name, a secondary name given to a person in classical Chinese culture.
  • 弥月 - Refers to the period of one month after childbirth, a significant time in traditional Chinese culture.
  • 女使 - Refers to a female attendant or servant, often used in classical texts to describe female servants.
  • 耄 - Refers to old age, often used in classical texts to describe elderly individuals.
  • 媪 - Refers to elderly women, often used in classical texts to describe elderly female servants.
  • 鼓乐充庭，贵戚如市 - Describes a lively and festive scene, with music filling the courtyard and nobles gathering like a market, indicating a significant social event.

Proper Nouns

  • 妾 - The speaker, a humble woman.
  • 小红 - The daughter's name.
  • 王 - Lady Wang, the host of the banquet.
  • 黄 - Huang, the guest invited to the banquet.

[1212]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 黄留三日始去 - Literal: 'Yellow (a name or title) stayed for three days before leaving.' The meaning of '黄' is unclear; it could be a name, a title, or a metaphorical reference. Rendered as 'Yellow' but note the uncertainty.
  • 逆女归宁 - Literal: 'To bring a daughter home' or 'to fetch a daughter back,' '归宁' means 'to return to one's parents' home.' Together, it likely refers to a daughter returning home to visit her parents.
  • 抱子于怀 - Literal: 'Hold the child in the bosom,' a common expression for carrying a child close to the body.
  • 要王相送 - '要' can mean 'to invite' or 'to request,' and '相送' means 'to accompany on the way.' Together, it suggests that the woman invited Wang to accompany her on her journey.
  • 寂无行人 - Literal: 'Silent and without people,' indicating a desolate area.
  • 屏人与语 - '屏人' means 'to shoo away people' or 'to ensure privacy,' and '与语' means 'to speak with.' Together, it indicates a private conversation.
  • 会短离长 - Poetic expression meaning 'short meetings and long partings,' often used to describe the bittersweet nature of relationships.
  • 感义而思所报 - '感义' means 'to be moved by righteousness,' and '思所报' means 'to think of how to repay.' Together, it suggests that the woman was moved by Wang's righteousness and wanted to repay him.
  • 借归宁，解儿危难 - '借归宁' means 'to use the pretext of returning home,' and '解儿危难' means 'to resolve the child's danger.' Together, it suggests that the woman used her return home as an excuse to resolve the child's difficulties.
  • 记取家有死口时 - '记取' means 'to remember,' '家有死口' can mean 'when there is a death in the family,' and '时' means 'at that time.' Together, it suggests that Wang should remember when there is a death in his family.
  • 晨鸡初唱 - Literal: 'The rooster's first crow,' a common expression for early morning.
  • 挑葵花灯来者 - '挑葵花灯' means 'carrying a lamp with a sunflower pattern,' and '来者' means 'the one who comes.' Together, it describes a person carrying a specific type of lamp.
  • 遮道苦求 - '遮道' means 'to block the road,' and '苦求' means 'to earnestly request.' Together, it suggests that the person earnestly requests something while blocking the road.
  • 临别，执手怆然交涕 - '临别' means 'at the moment of parting,' '执手' means 'to hold hands,' '怆然' means 'with sorrow,' and '交涕' means 'to shed tears together.' Together, it describes a tearful farewell.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 黄 - The meaning of '黄' is unclear. It could be a name, a title, or a metaphorical reference. For now, rendered as 'Yellow' but note the uncertainty.
  • 归宁 - A traditional Chinese custom where a married daughter returns to her parents' home to visit.
  • 葵花灯 - A type of traditional Chinese lamp with a sunflower pattern, often used in folklore and literature.
  • 晨鸡初唱 - A common expression in Chinese literature to indicate early morning.

Proper Nouns

  • 黄 - The meaning of '黄' is unclear. It could be a name, a title, or a metaphorical reference. For now, rendered as 'Yellow' but note the uncertainty.
  • 王 - The name 'Wang' is a proper noun and should be consistently rendered as 'Wang' throughout the translation.
  • 江南 - A geographical term referring to southern China, which should be consistently rendered as 'southern China' or 'Jiangnan' throughout the translation.
  • 西河柳堤 - A specific location, which should be consistently rendered as 'West River Willow Embankment' or 'Xiheliuditai' throughout the translation.

[1213]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 經六七年，絕無音問 - Common classical expression indicating a long period of silence or lack of communication.
  • 死者甚眾 - Standard way to indicate a large number of deaths.
  • 曩嘱 - Classical expression meaning 'former instructions' or 'previous commands.'
  • 聞雞鳴 - Classical idiom often used to indicate the time of early morning or the beginning of a day.
  • 止隔百步許，愈追愈遠 - Common expression in classical Chinese to show a paradoxical or frustrating situation.
  • 懊恨而返 - Classical way to express emotional distress and return.
  • 數日暴病，尋卒 - '暴病' refers to a sudden or severe illness, and '尋卒' indicates a short time before death.
  • 無賴 - Term for idle or wicked people.
  • 公然 - Classical term meaning 'openly' or 'without shame.'
  • 陵替 - Classical expression for decline or deterioration.
  • 殇 - Classical term for a child who died before reaching adulthood.
  • 无所主 - Classical expression meaning 'no one in charge' or 'without a leader.'
  • 益横 - Idiom meaning 'increasingly arrogant' or 'more reckless.'
  • 俱空 - Classical expression for 'all empty' or 'completely gone.'
  • 瓜分 - Classical idiom meaning 'to divide up like watermelons,' often used to describe the partition of property among multiple people.
  • 鬻 - Classical term for 'to sell,' often used in the context of selling people into slavery or servitude.
  • 环泣 - Classical expression for 'crying in a circle' or 'crying together.'
  • 惨动 - Classical expression for 'moved by sorrow' or 'stirred by grief.'
  • 肩舆 - Classical term for a palanquin or litter.
  • 纷如市 - Classical expression meaning 'like a crowd in the market' or 'crowded with people.'
  • 天数 - Classical expression referring to fate or the will of Heaven.
  • 耽延 - Classical term for 'delayed' or 'postponed.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 王 - Refers to a king or noble figure.
  • 妾 - A concubine or low-status wife, often used in classical Chinese to denote a woman in a subordinate marital relationship.
  • 第宅 - Refers to a mansion or residence, often associated with the aristocracy or wealthy families.
  • 无赖 - A term used to describe idle, wicked, or morally corrupt individuals.
  • 殇 - A term from ancient Chinese funeral rites, referring to children who died before reaching adulthood.
  • 天数 - A concept in classical Chinese philosophy and literature, referring to the will of Heaven or fate.

Proper Nouns

  • 保儿 - Likely the name of a child, possibly the daughter of the妾.
  • 黄公 - A proper noun, likely the name of an official or elder figure. The term '公' is a title of respect, often used for officials or elders.

[1214]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不谓转盼间已成丘墟 - Colloquial expression meaning 'who would have thought that in the blink of an eye it had become a ruin.'
  • 欷歔 - Onomatopoeic term for sobbing or sighing.
  • 登簿记名 - Bureaucratic term meaning 'to record on a register and note names.'
  • 械禁严迫 - Legal term meaning 'to imprison and enforce strict punishment.'
  • 非慕贞体胤 - Formal expression meaning 'not out of admiration for chastity and lineage.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丘墟 - Refers to a ruined place, often used metaphorically to describe the aftermath of destruction or loss.
  • 黄公 - Likely a reference to a respected elder or authority figure, possibly a family patriarch or community leader.

  • The sequence '公行二三里，又遣隶回，视其所入何村' describes the official's continued journey and his instructions to his subordinate.
  • The clause '隶尾之，至一村舍，两男子迎之而入，还以白公' describes the investigation and the report back to the official.
  • The dialogue '公谓其邑宰：“城中得无有劫寇否？”宰曰：“无之。”' reflects the official's concern and the magistrate's response.
  • The clause '时功令严，上下讳盗，故即被盗贼劫杀，亦隐忍而不敢言' explains the social and legal context of the time.
  • The sequence '公就馆舍，嘱家人细访之，果有富室被强寇入室，炮烙而死' describes the official's actions and the outcome of the investigation.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sequence of actions in '张视几上有《南华经注》，因取就枕上伏榻翻阅' must be read as a single, continuous action: Zhang looked around, saw the *Nanhua Jing* commentary on the table, took it, lay it on the pillow, and began to read it.
  • The sudden entrance of Shunhua in '忽舜华推扉入' must be read as an abrupt interruption of Zhang's reading.
  • Zhang's actions of putting down the book and searching for his hat and shoes in '张释卷，搜觅冠履' must be read as a reaction to Shunhua's entrance.
  • The woman's quick sitting and speaking in '女即榻捷坐曰' must be read as a direct response to Zhang's actions.
  • The complex sentence in '妾以君风流才士，欲以门户相托，遂犯瓜李之嫌' must be read as a single, coherent statement expressing the woman's intentions and the potential risk involved.
  • The question in '得不相遐弃否' must be read as a plea for reassurance.
  • Zhang's state of being at a loss in '张皇然不知所对' must be read as a reaction to the woman's words.
  • Zhang's response in '但云，小生家中固有妻耳' must be read as a straightforward admission of his marital status.
  • The woman's response in '此亦见君诚笃，顾亦不妨' must be read as a reassurance and a suggestion to proceed.
  • The conditional statement in '既不嫌憎，明日当烦媒妁' must be read as a plan for the future.
  • The sequence of actions in '言已欲去，张探身挽之，女亦遂留' must be read as a single, continuous interaction.
  • The woman's early morning actions in '未曙即起，以金赠张' must be read as a gesture of goodwill.
  • The woman's instructions in '君持作临眺之资，向暮宜晚来，恐旁人所窥' must be read as a plan for Zhang's future visits.
  • Zhang's actions and the routine in '张如其言，早出晏归，半年以为常' must be read as a continuation of their relationship.

Proper Nouns

  • 张 - Zhang, the male protagonist.
  • 舜华 - Shunhua, the female protagonist.
  • 南华经注 - Commentary on the *Nanhua Jing* (Zhuangzi).

  • 江邊柳下三弄笛，抛向江心莫叹息 - Three times playing the flute by the willow by the river, throw it into the river and do not lament, a poetic and enigmatic instruction.
  • 因便叩请 - Taking the opportunity to ask respectfully, a polite way of making a request.
  • 我何能疗病？请为三弄可也 - How could I cure your illness? Please let me play the flute three times, a humble yet confident response.
  • 拜求益切，且倾囊献之 - He bowed and requested more earnestly, offering everything he had, a gesture of deep gratitude and respect.
  • 金在江边，请自取之 - The gold is on the riverbank; please take it yourself, an instruction that seems to test the dreamer's understanding.
  • 君未能恝然耶？ - Are you still unable to let go? a rhetorical question challenging the dreamer's attachment.
  • 俗哉！ - How vulgar! a sharp rebuke that seems to provoke a reaction.
  • 公受拍，张吻作声，喉中呕出一物，堕地然堛，俯而破之，赤丝中裹饭犹存，病若失 - The official was struck, opened his mouth and made a sound, vomited something from his throat, which fell to the ground and was broken open, revealing rice wrapped in red silk, and his illness seemed to vanish, a vivid description of a miraculous cure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道士 - Taoist priest, a figure often associated with spiritual and healing practices in Chinese culture.
  • 仙人 - Immortal, a being of higher spiritual status, often associated with longevity and supernatural abilities.
  • 河岳 - Rivers and mountains, symbols of natural strength and endurance, often used to describe noble or enduring qualities.

  • 天仙 - Heavenly Immortal, a being of even higher spiritual status, often associated with divine intervention and miraculous powers.

  • 路经深山，被曳近削壁，将推堕 - 路经 means 'pass through,' 削壁 means 'steep cliff,' and 将推堕 means 'about to push down'; describes the prisoner being dragged near a steep cliff and about to be pushed off.

  • 见云萝扶婢出，凄然慰吊曰 - 见云萝扶婢出 means 'saw Yun Luo being supported by a maid,' 凄然 means 'grief-stricken,' 慰吊 means 'to comfort and mourn,' and 曰 means 'said'; describes the prisoner seeing Yun Luo being supported by a maid and offering comfort.

  • 因取生胸前带，连结十余扣，嘱云 - 因取生胸前带 means 'took the prisoner's belt,' 连结十余扣 means 'tied it into several knots,' and 嘱云 means 'instructed him to say'; describes Yun Luo tying the prisoner's belt and giving him instructions.
  • 见官时，拈此结而解之，可以弭祸 - 见官时 means 'when you see the official,' 拈此结而解之 means 'untie this knot,' and 可以弭祸 means 'you can avoid disaster'; gives the prisoner specific instructions to avoid danger.

[1273]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 異史氏曰 - Phrase used by the author of *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio* to introduce a commentary or moral reflection.
  • 悍妻妒婦 - Refers to a domineering wife and a jealous woman, respectively.
  • 疽附於骨 - Metaphor for a deeply rooted and painful problem, like a tumor attached to bone.
  • 死而後已 - Means 'until death,' emphasizing the persistence of the problem.
  • 豈不毒哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how toxic is it not?'
  • 砒、附 - Refers to arsenic and aconite, both highly toxic substances.
  • 瞑眩大瘳 - Medical term meaning 'a sudden and dramatic improvement,' often used metaphorically for a significant positive change.
  • 非參、苓所能及矣 - Means 'not something that can be achieved by ginseng or poria,' referring to the limitations of conventional remedies.
  • 仙人洞見臟腑 - Refers to a mythical ability to see internal organs, symbolizing profound insight or understanding.
  • 烏敢以毒藥貽子孫哉 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how dare one leave toxic substances to one's descendants?'
  • 少倜儻不泥 - Describes someone as young, free-spirited, and unbound by convention.
  • 絲竹詞曲之屬皆精之 - Means 'all types of music and poetry are mastered.'
  • 甲榜 - Refers to the top rank in the imperial examination.
  • 孝廉益佻脫 - Means 'the scholar-official became even more carefree and unrestrained.'
  • 稍稍禁制之 - Means 'to try to restrain him a little.'
  • 亡去 - Means 'to run away.'
  • 三年不返 - Means 'did not return for three years.'
  • 臨清勾欄中 - Refers to a brothel in Linqing.
  • 南向坐 - Means 'sitting facing south,' a position of honor.
  • 少姬十數左右侍 - Means 'dozens of young concubines were serving him.'
  • 學音藝而拜門牆者也 - Means 'those who learned music and art under his tutelage.'
  • 積衣累笥 - Means 'a pile of clothes in a chest.'
  • 悉諸姬所貽 - Means 'all given by the concubines.'
  • 閉置一室 - Means 'confined to a room.'
  • 投書滿案 - Means 'letters scattered on the desk.'
  • 長繩系榻足 - Means 'a long rope tied to the foot of the bed.'
  • 貫以巨鈴 - Means 'passed through a large bell.'
  • 系諸廚下 - Means 'tied to the kitchen.'
  • 凡有所需則蹑繩 - Means 'whenever he needed something, he would step on the rope.'
  • 繩動鈴響則應之 - Means 'the rope moving would make the bell ring, and she would respond.'
  • 躬設典肆 - Means 'she personally set up a pawnshop.'
  • 垂簾納物而估其直 - Means 'she hung a curtain to receive items and appraised their value.'
  • 左持籌，右握管 - Means 'holding a counting stick in her left hand and a pen in her right.'
  • 老僕供奔走而已 - Means 'an old servant ran errands for her.'
  • 居積致富 - Means 'became wealthy through hoarding and trade.'
  • 恥不及諸姒貴 - Means 'ashamed that she was not as prestigious as her sisters-in-law.'
  • 三卵兩成 - Metaphor for three eggs producing only two chickens, suggesting disappointment or failure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 異史氏 - The author of *Strange Tales from a Chinese Studio*, Pu Songling.
  • 悍妻妒婦 - Common archetypes in Chinese literature representing difficult and jealous women.
  • 砒、附 - Highly toxic substances, often used in traditional Chinese medicine but also symbolizing danger.
  • 參、苓 - Ginseng and poria, common herbs in traditional Chinese medicine, symbolizing conventional remedies.
  • 仙人洞見臟腑 - A mythical ability, symbolizing profound insight.
  • 臨清 - A city in Shandong Province, known for its cultural and economic significance.
  • 勾欄 - A brothel or a place of entertainment.
  • 絲竹詞曲 - Refers to music and poetry, important cultural elements in Chinese society.
  • 甲榜 - The highest rank in the imperial examination, indicating high social status.
  • 孝廉 - A scholar-official, a person who passed the imperial examination and held a government position.
  • 典肆 - A pawnshop, a common business in traditional Chinese society.
  • 垂簾 - A curtain, often used in traditional Chinese settings to create privacy.
  • 籌 - A counting stick, used for calculations.
  • 管 - A pen, used for writing.

Proper Nouns

[1274]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 又耿進士崧生 - Structure '又...進士' introduces a new subject, possibly another example or character.
  • 夫人每以績火佐讀 - '績火' is a compound meaning 'spinning by firelight' or 'spinning while lighting the fire,' indicating a domestic activity performed to support his studies.
  • 績者不辍，讀者不敢息也 - Parallel structure emphasizing mutual discipline and support.
  • 或朋舊相詣 - '朋舊' refers to old friends or former associates.
  • 辄竊聽之 - '竊聽' is a euphemism for eavesdropping or listening in without permission.
  • 論文則淪茗作黍 - '淪茗' is a poetic way of saying 'drink tea,' and '作黍' refers to preparing millet, a metaphor for a quiet, scholarly conversation.
  • 每試得平等，不敢入室門 - Indicates shame or humility in his performance.
  • 超等始笑迎之 - Only when he passed with the highest grade would his wife greet him with a smile.
  • 設帳得金悉內獻 - '設帳' refers to teaching in a private school or tutoring.
  • 丝毫不敢匿 - Emphasizes complete honesty and transparency.
  • 故東主饋遺，恒面較锱銖 - '锱銖' are ancient units of weight, used here metaphorically to mean 'even the smallest amount.'
  • 人或非笑之 - '非笑' can mean to criticize, mock, or laugh at.
  • 而不知其銷算良難也 - Suggests that the wife was managing the finances with great care and difficulty.
  • 後為婦翁延教內弟 - '婦翁' is 'father-in-law,' and '內弟' is 'son-in-law’s younger brother' or 'nephew by marriage.'
  • 是年游泮 - '游泮' refers to passing the county-level examination (the first step in the imperial examination system).
  • 翁謝儀十金 - '謝儀' is a formal term for a gift or offering.
  • 耿受盒返金 - A formal act of returning a gift.
  • 夫人知之曰 - His wife learned of this and said...
  • 彼雖固親，然舌耕為何也？ - '舌耕' is a metaphor for teaching, meaning 'plowing with the tongue' (earning a living through speech).
  • 追之返而受之 - She chased after him and took it back.
  • 耿不敢爭，而心終歉焉 - Geng dared not argue, but his heart remained full of guilt.
  • 思暗償之 - He resolved to secretly repay it.
  • 每歲館金，皆短其數以報夫人 - Each year, he would shortchange the amount of the teaching fee to repay his wife.
  • 積二年餘得若干數 - After accumulating for over two years, he had gathered a certain amount.
  • 忽夢一人告之曰 - Suddenly, he had a dream in which a person told him...
  • 明日登高，金數即滿 - '登高' can mean climbing a hill or ascending, and here it may be a metaphor or literal instruction.
  • 次日試一臨眺 - The next day, he tried to look out from a high place.
  • 果拾遺金 - Indeed, he found a lost sum of money.
  • 恰符缺數 - Exactly matching the missing amount.
  • 遂償岳 - Thus, he repaid his father-in-law.
  • 後成進士 - Later, he became a jinshi (進士), the highest degree in the imperial examination.
  • 夫人猶呵谴之 - His wife still scolded him.
  • 鳥語 - May be a concluding phrase or a marker for the end of the text. It could be a title or an indicator that the text is a fable or allegory. '鳥語' literally means 'bird language' or 'the language of birds,' and may be symbolic or a literary device.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 設帳 - Teaching in a private school or tutoring, a common way to earn a living during the imperial era.
  • 游泮 - Passing the county-level examination, the first stage in the imperial examination system.
  • 舌耕 - A metaphor for earning a living through teaching or speaking, i.e., 'plowing with the tongue.'
  • 婦翁 - The father-in-law, an important family figure in traditional Chinese society.
  • 內弟 - The son-in-law’s younger brother or a nephew by marriage.
  • 謝儀 - A formal gift or offering, often given as a token of appreciation.
  • 岳 - A term for father-in-law or the father of one’s wife.
  • 宰相 - The prime minister or chief minister in the imperial bureaucracy.
  • 諺云 - A proverb or saying, used to convey moral or practical wisdom.

[1275]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 募食乡村 - Means 'to solicit food in the countryside,' indicating the Taoist's practice of traveling and seeking alms.
  • 因告主人使慎火 - Means 'therefore, he warned the host to be cautious with fire,' showing the Taoist's concern for safety.
  • 鸟云 - Means 'the bird says,' indicating a reported speech.
  • 大火难救，可怕 - Means 'a big fire is hard to save, it's frightening,' expressing the bird's warning.
  • 众笑之，竟不备 - Means 'the crowd laughed at him, but did not prepare,' showing the villagers' dismissal of the warning.
  • 明日果火，延烧数家 - Means 'the next day, a fire broke out, burning several houses,' confirming the bird's warning.
  • 始惊其神 - Means 'they were finally shocked by his supernatural ability,' indicating the villagers' realization of the Taoist's foresight.
  • 好事者追及之，称为仙 - Means 'curious people caught up with him and called him a deity,' showing the villagers' reaction to the Taoist's ability.
  • 我不过知鸟语耳，何仙乎 - Means 'I only know bird language, how can I be a deity?' indicating the Taoist's modesty.
  • 适有皂花雀鸣树上 - Means 'just then, a black and white sparrow was singing on a tree,' introducing a new bird.
  • 初六养之，初六养之；十四、十六殇之 - Means 'nurture them on the sixth day, nurture them on the sixth day; they will die on the fourteenth and sixteenth days,' indicating the bird's prophecy.
  • 想其家双生矣 - Means 'I think there are twins in the family,' showing the Taoist's deduction.
  • 不出五六日，当俱死也 - Means 'they will both die within five or six days,' indicating the bird's prediction.
  • 询之果生二子，无何并死，其日悉符 - Means 'when asked, it turned out they had two sons, and they both died soon after, and the days matched exactly,' confirming the bird's prophecy.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist practitioner, indicating the cultural context of Taoism in China.
  • 募食乡村 - A practice of Taoists and monks to travel and seek alms, reflecting the religious and social practices of the time.
  • 鸟语 - The ability to understand and interpret the language of birds, often associated with supernatural or mystical abilities in Chinese folklore.
  • 皂花雀 - A type of sparrow, often used in Chinese literature to symbolize wisdom or prophecy.
  • 双生 - Refers to twins, a concept that has various cultural and symbolic meanings in Chinese tradition.
  • 殇之 - Refers to the death of young children, a term used in ancient Chinese texts to describe the death of children before adulthood.

Proper Nouns

  • 道士 - Refers to a Taoist practitioner.
  • 皂花雀 - Refers to a black and white sparrow.

[1276]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 罢罢！偏向他！ - Enough! It favors him; colloquial expression used by the ducks to mock the magistrate's household disputes.
  • 盖妻妾反唇，令适被喧聒而出也 - It is because the concubines are quarreling, and the magistrate just happened to be disturbed and came out.
  • 因留居署中，优礼之 - He then kept him in the office, treating him with great respect.
  • 辨鸟言，多奇中 - He could distinguish bird language, often hitting the mark.
  • 道士朴野多肆言，辄无顾忌 - The Taoist was simple and rustic, often speaking freely without restraint.
  • 令最贪，一切供用诸物，皆折为钱以入之 - The magistrate was extremely greedy, converting all supplies and goods into money for himself.
  • 今日所言，不与前同，乃为明公会计耳 - What I am saying today is different from before; it is an account for the honorable gentleman.
  • 彼云：‘蜡烛一百八，银朱一千八。’ - They say, 'One hundred and eight candles, one thousand and eight vermilion'; cryptic message interpreted as a warning about the magistrate's corruption.
  • 令惭，疑其相讥 - The magistrate felt ashamed and suspected he was being mocked.
  • 道士求去，不许 - The Taoist asked to leave, but was not allowed.
  • 未几令果以墨败 - Soon after, the magistrate indeed failed due to corruption.
  • 邑有父子，俱青、社生，将赴岁试，忽有蝉落襟上 - In the county, there was a father and son, both born in Qing and She, who were about to take the annual examination when suddenly a cicada fell on their clothes.
  • 稍迁，吉兆也 - Slightly promoted, a good omen.
  • 何物稍迁，都了而已 - What kind of slightly promoted is this, just all done; sarcastic response foreshadowing the outcome.
  • 已而果皆被黜 - Soon after, they were both dismissed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 邑令 - Refers to the local magistrate, a common official in ancient Chinese society.
  • 青、社生 - Refers to the sons of Qing and She, indicating their social status or family background.
  • 岁试 - Refers to the annual examination, a significant event in the lives of scholars.
  • 齐俗 - Refers to the customs of the Qi region, adding a regional flavor to the story.
  • 蝉 - In Chinese culture, cicadas are often associated with change and transformation, but here they are used as a metaphor for success and failure.

Proper Nouns

Cultural and Historical Allusions

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '偶入郡，飲醉過市' describes a sequence of events: entering the prefecture, getting drunk, and passing through the market. The order and timing of these events are clear but need to be conveyed naturally in English.
  • The placement of '聞高門中有箫声' is important. It indicates that the sound of the flute was heard while passing through the market, adding a sensory detail to the narrative.
  • The clause '詢知為開壽筵者，然門庭殊清寂' provides additional information about the scene. It explains that the person inquired and found out it was a birthday banquet, but the courtyard and entrance were unusually quiet and desolate. The contrast between the expected celebration and the actual quietness is significant.

  • Sentences like '迟之三十年兴方至' and '面质之' must be read as single, continuous periods and actions, respectively.
  • Sentences such as '以草菅人命罚作畜' and '稽某所为，曾挞其父母，其罪维均' must be read as single, continuous punishments and investigations, respectively.
  • Sentences like '阎罗不即拘，待其禄尽' and '某生于顺天府市肆中' must be read as single, continuous delays and events, respectively.
  • Sentences such as '一日卧街头，适有客自南携金毛犬来' and '大如狸，心易其小，龁之' must be read as single, continuous encounters and sequences of actions, respectively.
  • Sentences like '小犬咬其喉下，系缀如铃' and '大犬摆扑嗥窜，市人解之不得，两犬俱毙' must be read as single, continuous sequences of events.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 惟受赃而枉法，真人面而兽心 - Introduces a judgment or condemnation: 'It is only that...' and uses the metaphor 'having the face of a human but the heart of a beast' to describe someone who appears virtuous but acts with cruelty or corruption.
  • 剔髓伐毛，暂罚冥死 - Hyperbolic expression 'to extract marrow and pluck hair,' metaphorically meaning to strip away one’s very essence or soul; 'temporary punishment of death in the afterlife.'
  • 脱皮换革，仍令胎生 - Metaphor for transformation or rebirth, possibly involving a change of form or nature; suggests being reborn in a new form, perhaps as an animal or lower being.
  • 隶役者：既在鬼曹，便非人类 - '隶役者' refers to a person who has been enslaved or bound to servitude, possibly in the afterlife; '既在鬼曹' means 'already in the realm of ghosts or spirits'; '便非人类' means 'they are no longer human.'
  • 只宜公门修行，庶还落蓐之身 - '公门' refers to the official or bureaucratic realm; '修行' means to cultivate virtue or perform penance; '庶还落蓐之身' means 'perhaps return to the body of a newborn' or 'perhaps be reborn as a human again.'
  • 何得苦海生波，益造弥天之孽？ - '苦海' is a metaphor for the suffering of the afterlife or the cycle of rebirth; '生波' means 'to create waves' or 'to cause disturbance'; '弥天之孽' means 'a sin that fills the sky' or 'a great sin that spreads across the heavens.'
  • 飞扬跋扈，狗脸生六月之霜 - '飞扬跋扈' is an idiom meaning 'arrogant and unrestrained' or 'domineering and uncontrolled'; '狗脸生六月之霜' is a metaphor implying an unnatural or cruel expression.
  • 隳突叫号，虎威断九衢之路 - '隳突' means to break through or destroy with violence; '叫号' is to cry out or howl; '虎威' refers to the power or dominance of a tiger; '断九衢之路' means 'to block the nine roads' or 'to disrupt the major thoroughfares.'
  • 肆淫威于冥界，咸知狱吏为尊 - '肆淫威' means 'to wield tyranny freely' or 'to act with unrestrained cruelty'; '冥界' is the underworld; '咸知狱吏为尊' means 'all know the jailer as the most respected' or 'all recognize the jailer as the highest authority.'
  • 助酷虐于昏官，共以屠伯是惧 - '助酷虐' means 'to assist in cruelty' or 'to aid in harsh punishment'; '昏官' refers to corrupt or incompetent officials; '屠伯' is a term for a butcher or executioner; '共以屠伯是惧' means 'all fear the executioner' or 'they all fear the butcher.'
  • 当以法场之内，剁其四肢 - '法场' is the place of execution or judicial punishment; '剁其四肢' means 'to chop off its limbs.'
  • 更向汤镬之中，捞其筋骨 - '汤镬' is a cauldron used for boiling, often in the context of execution; '捞其筋骨' means 'to fish out its bones and sinews.'
  • 余腥犹能役鬼，大力直可通神 - '余腥' refers to the lingering foulness or corruption; '役鬼' means 'to command ghosts'; '大力直可通神' means 'his great power can directly reach the gods.'
  • 宜籍羊氏之家，以偿席生之孝 - '籍' here likely means to confiscate or appropriate; '羊氏之家' refers to the family of Yang; '偿席生之孝' means 'to repay the filial piety of Xi Sheng.'
  • 即押赴东岳施行 - '押赴' means 'to send to' or 'to escort to'; '东岳' is the Eastern Peak, associated with judgment and punishment; '施行' means 'to carry out' or 'to execute.'
  • 念汝子孝义，汝性良懦，可再赐阳寿三纪 - '念汝子孝义' means 'I consider your son’s filial piety and righteousness'; '汝性良懦' means 'you are naturally gentle or timid'; '阳寿' is the lifespan granted by the heavens; '三纪' is a unit of time, traditionally 36 years.
  • 席乃抄其判词，途中父子共读之 - '抄其判词' means 'to copy down the verdict or judgment'; '共读之' means 'to read it together.'
  • 既至家，席先苏：令家人启棺视父，僵尸犹冰，俟之终日，渐温而活 - '席先苏' means 'Xi awoke first'; '启棺视父' means 'to open the coffin and look at his father'; '僵尸犹冰' means 'the corpse was still like ice'; '俟之终日' means 'waited for a whole day'; '渐温而活' means 'gradually warmed and came back to life.'
  • 又索抄词，则已无矣 - '索抄词' means 'to search for the copied verdict'; '则已无矣' means 'but it was already gone.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 冥界 - The underworld or afterlife in Chinese cosmology.
  • 鬼曹 - A term for the realm of spirits or the domain of the dead.
  • 公门 - The domain of justice, law, or the bureaucratic system.
  • 东岳 - The Eastern Peak, often associated with Mount Tai, and in Daoist cosmology, one of the four great mountains, associated with the god of the east and the heavens.
  • 阳寿 - The life granted by the heavens, as opposed to the life in the underworld.
  • 三纪 - A unit of time in classical Chinese, traditionally 36 years (3 × 12 years per '纪').
  • 席生 - A name, likely 'Xi Sheng,' referring to the son who performed filial piety.
  • 羊某 - A name, likely 'Yang,' referring to the corrupt official.
  • 阎摩殿 - The Hall of Yama, the god of death in Indian and Chinese cosmology.
  • 枉死城 - The City of the Wrongly Dead, a place in the afterlife for those who died unjustly.
  • 东岳施行 - Refers to the execution or judgment carried out by the god of the Eastern Peak, often associated with the underworld or divine justice.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 吾两人情虽如胞，实非同族 - Our feelings for each other are like those of siblings, but we are not of the same clan, highlighting a deep emotional bond despite a lack of blood relation.
  • 弟自分已登鬼箓 - I have already entered the register of the dead, indicating a sense of impending death or resignation.
  • 衔恩无可相报 - I hold your kindness but have no way to repay it, expressing gratitude and helplessness.
  • 素秋已长成，既蒙嫂抚爱，媵之可也 - Suqiu has grown up, and since she has been raised by you, she can be given away as a concubine, suggesting a practical and somewhat cold-hearted decision.
  • 公子作色曰 - The young master changed his expression and said, indicating a shift in mood or attitude.
  • 是真吾弟之乱命也 - This is truly my brother's rash command, expressing disapproval or frustration.
  • 其将谓我人头畜鸣者耶 - Will they think I am a man with a human head but the voice of an animal? expressing a sense of insult or indignation.
  • 恂九泣下 - Xunjiu wept, indicating deep emotion or sorrow.
  • 公子即以重金为购良材 - The young master immediately used a large sum of money to buy good wood, suggesting a practical and immediate action.
  • 恂九命舁至，力疾而入 - Xunjiu ordered it to be carried there and entered with great effort, indicating a struggle or determination.
  • 我没后即阖棺，无令一人开视 - After I die, close the coffin immediately and do not let anyone open it, expressing a final, firm instruction.
  • 公子尚欲有言，而目已瞑矣 - The young master still wanted to say something, but his eyes had already closed, indicating the finality of death.
  • 公子哀伤，如丧手足 - The young master mourned deeply, as if he had lost a limb, expressing profound grief.
  • 然窃疑其嘱异 - However, he secretly suspected that his instructions were unusual, indicating a sense of unease or doubt.
  • 俟素秋他出，启而视之 - After Suqiu left, he opened it to see, suggesting a moment of curiosity or concern.
  • 棺中袍服如蜕 - The clothes inside the coffin were like shed skin, indicating a strange or unnatural appearance.
  • 揭之，有蠹鱼径尺僵卧其中 - When he lifted them, there was a dead bookworm a foot long lying stiffly inside, describing a bizarre and unsettling discovery.
  • 骇异间，素秋促入 - In shock, Suqiu hurriedly entered, indicating a sudden and unexpected event.
  • 惨然曰 - Said with a sad expression, expressing sorrow or regret.
  • 兄弟何所隔阂 - What is there to separate brothers? expressing a sense of unity or reconciliation.
  • 所以然者非避兄也 - The reason for this is not to avoid my brother, indicating a rationalization or explanation.
  • 但恐传布飞扬，妾亦不能久居耳 - But I fear that the news will spread, and I cannot stay for long, expressing concern about potential consequences.
  • 礼缘情制，情之所在，异族何殊焉 - Rituals are based on emotions, and where emotions exist, what difference does kinship make? expressing a philosophical or moral perspective.
  • 妹宁不知我心乎 - Sister, don't you know my heart? expressing a sense of intimacy or trust.
  • 即中馈当无漏言，请勿虑 - Even the kitchen will not leak any words, please do not worry, indicating a promise of secrecy or discretion.
  • 遂速卜吉期，厚葬之 - So he quickly chose a good time and buried him with great respect, indicating a practical and respectful action.
  • 初，公子欲以素秋论婚于世家 - Initially, the young master wanted to arrange Suqiu's marriage to a prominent family, suggesting a previous plan or intention.
  • 恂九不欲 - Xunjiu did not want to, indicating a disagreement or reluctance.
  • 既殁，公子商于素秋，素秋不应 - After his death, the young master discussed it with Suqiu, but she did not respond, indicating a lack of communication or resolution.

[1360]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 是秋入闱复落 - This autumn, entering the examination hall again and failing. *闱* refers to the examination hall or imperial examination. *复落* means to fail again.
  • 邑邑不得志 - Compound idiom meaning 'disheartened and unable to achieve one's ambitions.' *邑邑* is a rare term, often used to express sorrow or dejection.
  • 颇思郎言 - 'He greatly thought of the master's words.' *颇* means 'quite' or 'much'; *思* is to think or recall; *郎言* is 'the master's words' or 'the teacher's words.'
  • 强读之 - 'Forced to read it.' *强* here means to force or compel oneself to do something.
  • 昏昏欲睡 - Common idiom meaning 'drowsy and on the verge of sleep.'
  • 心惶惑无以自主 - 'His heart was in confusion and he could not control himself.' *惶惑* means confused or anxious; *无以自主* means 'unable to take control.'
  • 场期将近 - 'The examination period was approaching.' *场期* is the time of the examination.
  • 出拟题七使贾作文 - 'He proposed seven topics and had [the student] write an essay.' *拟题* is to propose or set a topic; *使贾作文* is to have [the student] write an essay.
  • 索文而阅，不以为可 - 'He read the essay and did not find it satisfactory.' *索文* is to retrieve or look for the essay; *不以为可* is 'did not think it acceptable.'
  • 又令复作 - 'He ordered [the student] to write it again.'
  • 訾之 - 'Criticized it.' *訾* is to criticize or censure.
  • 贾戏于落卷中 - 'Jia played with the failed exam papers.' *戏* is to play or joke; *落卷* is the discarded or failed exam papers.
  • 集其葛茸泛滥，不可告人之句，连缀成文 - 'Collected the vague, rambling, and indecent sentences and strung them together into an essay.' *葛茸泛滥* is a metaphor for vague and incoherent writing; *不可告人* is 'not fit to be told to others,' i.e., indecent or shameful.
  • 得之矣 - 'Got it!' or 'Found it!' A colloquial expression of realization.
  • 因使熟记，坚嘱勿忘 - 'Then he ordered him to memorize it thoroughly and sternly warned him not to forget.' *熟记* is to memorize thoroughly; *坚嘱* is to strongly advise.
  • 实相告 - 'To tell the truth.' A formal expression used to introduce a candid statement.
  • 此言不由中 - 'These words are not from the heart.' *不由中* means not sincere or not coming from the heart.
  • 转瞬即去 - 'Disappeared in an instant.' *转瞬* is 'in the blink of an eye.'
  • 便受夏楚，不能复忆之也 - 'But immediately after, he was subjected to punishment and could not remember it again.' *夏楚* is a term for corporal punishment; *复忆* is to recall again.
  • 坐案头，强令自诵一遍 - 'Sat at the desk and ordered him to recite it once.' *坐案头* is to sit at the writing desk; *强令* is to command or compel.
  • 袒背 - 'To expose the back,' a gesture for punishment or ritual.
  • 以笔写符而去 - 'Wrote a talisman with a brush and left.' *符* is a talisman or magical script used in Daoist or folk practices.
  • 只此已足，可以束阁群书矣 - 'This alone is enough; you may now lock away all your books.' *束阁* is to lock away or store away; *群书* is all the books.
  • 验其符，濯之不下，深入肌理 - 'When the talisman was tested, it could not be washed off and had penetrated the flesh.' *验其符* is to test the talisman; *濯之不下* is 'could not be washed off'; *深入肌理* is 'penetrated the flesh and sinews.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 入闱 - Refers to participating in the imperial examination, a major institution in the Chinese civil service system. The *闱* was the secure examination hall.
  • 场期 - The time of the imperial examination, a major event in the life of a scholar-official aspirant.
  • 郎 - A respectful term for a teacher or mentor, possibly a Daoist or Confucian scholar.
  • 夏楚 - A term for corporal punishment, often used in classical texts to refer to the beating with a *夏* (a type of rod) or *楚* (a type of whip).
  • 符 - A talisman or magical script, often used in Daoist or folk religious practices to invoke supernatural power or protection.
  • 束阁群书 - A metaphor for the idea that once the correct knowledge is obtained, further study is unnecessary. It reflects a Daoist or Confucian ideal of internalizing knowledge.
  • 濯之不下，深入肌理 - This phrase is reminiscent of Daoist or folk magic where talismans are believed to have a physical and spiritual effect on the body.

  • 乍见甚惊 - Describes the immediate reaction to a startling event, reflecting the human tendency to be surprised by unexpected occurrences.
  • 因忆师言，收神凝坐 - Reflects the importance of mindfulness and concentration in spiritual practice, as well as the role of a teacher in guiding the student.
  • 虎似知有其人，寻入近榻 - Describes the tiger's behavior, suggesting a deeper understanding or awareness of the human presence, which is a common theme in Chinese literature involving animals.

[1373]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 官拘鄂 - Standard narrative phrase indicating arrest; 'Officials detained Wu.'

  • 见人羞涩如童子 - Describes shyness or bashfulness; *羞涩* conveys 'shy' or 'bashful.'
  • 被执骇绝 - Means 'when seized, he was terrified beyond measure'; *骇绝* implies extreme fear.
  • 上堂不能置词 - Means 'when brought before the court, he could not speak'; *置词* means 'to state or argue one’s case.'
  • 惟有战栗 - Means 'only shivering'; *战栗* is a strong term for trembling or shaking from fear.
  • 宰益信其情实 - Means 'the magistrate became more convinced of his guilt'; *宰* refers to the local magistrate or judge.
  • 横加梏械 - Means 'he was cruelly bound with shackles'; *梏械* are restraints or chains.
  • 生不堪痛楚，遂诬服 - Means 'he could not endure the pain and so falsely confessed'; *诬服* is 'falsely admitting guilt.'
  • 及解郡，敲扑如邑 - Means 'when he was sent to the county, he was beaten as before'; *敲扑* refers to beating or corporal punishment.
  • 生冤气填塞 - Means 'his grievance or injustice filled his heart'; *冤气* is a term for injustice or resentment.
  • 每欲与女面质 - Means 'he often wanted to confront the woman face to face'; *面质* means 'to confront or question someone in person.'
  • 及相见，女辄诟詈 - Means 'when they met, the woman would scold him'; *诟詈* is to scold or curse.
  • 遂结舌不能自伸 - Means 'he became silent and could not defend himself'; *结舌* is to be unable to speak, often due to fear or shame.
  • 由是论死 - Means 'as a result, he was sentenced to death'; *论死* is to be condemned to death.
  • 经数官复讯无异 - Means 'after several officials reviewed the case, there was no change in the verdict'; *复讯* is to re-examine or re-investigate.
  • 后委济南府复审 - Means 'later, the case was sent to the governor of Jinan for re-examination'; *委* means 'to entrust or assign.'
  • 时吴公南岱守济南 - Means 'at that time, Master Wu Nandai was the governor of Jinan'; *守* is the official title for a prefect or governor.
  • 一见鄂生，疑其不类杀人者 - Means 'upon seeing Wu, he suspected that he was not like a murderer'; *不类杀人者* is 'not like a killer' or 'not the type of person who would kill.'
  • 阴使人从容私问之 - Means 'he secretly sent someone to ask him privately and at ease'; *从容私问* is to question in a relaxed and discreet manner.
  • 俾尽得其词 - Means 'so that he could fully express his words'; *俾* is a classical particle meaning 'so that' or 'to cause.'
  • 公以是益知鄂生冤 - Means 'the magistrate thus became even more aware of Wu’s innocence'; *以是* means 'therefore' or 'because of this.'
  • 筹思数日始鞫之 - Means 'he pondered for several days before interrogating him'; *筹思* is to deliberate or think carefully.
  • 遇鄂生时别有人否？ - Means 'was there anyone else present when you met Wu?'; *别有人* means 'other people.'
  • 乃唤生上，温语慰问 - Means 'then he called Wu forward and spoke to him gently in a comforting manner'; *温语* is gentle speech.
  • 曾过其门，但见旧邻妇王氏同一少女出，某即趋避，过此并无一言 - Means 'I passed by her door and only saw an old neighbor’s wife, Wang, with a young girl coming out; I immediately turned away, and after that I did not speak to anyone'; *趋避* is to turn away or avoid.
  • 欲刑之 - Means 'he wanted to punish her'; *刑* is punishment or corporal punishment.
  • 公罢质，命拘王氏 - Means 'the magistrate stopped the interrogation and ordered Wang to be detained'; *罢质* is to stop questioning or end an interrogation.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 官拘鄂 - Narrative technique common in classical Chinese fiction, where the story begins with the arrest of a character.

  • 罢质 - Term used to describe the end of an interrogation or questioning.

[1406]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 父既归，坚辞欲去 - The father has returned home, and [the son] firmly declines to leave. This is a polite and formal way of expressing refusal.
  • 兄弟不忍 - The brothers cannot bear [the situation]. This implies a strong emotional reaction.
  • 析产而三之 - To divide the property into three parts. This is a formal expression used in legal or familial contexts.
  • 子得二，女得一也 - The sons receive two parts, and the daughter receives one part. This is a specific distribution of inheritance.
  • 大娘固辞 - The eldest sister firmly declines. This is another polite and formal way of expressing refusal.
  • 吾等非姊，乌有今日 - Without our elder sister, we would not be where we are today. This is a rhetorical statement emphasizing gratitude.
  • 福禄闻之皆流涕 - Hearing this, they all wept. This indicates a strong emotional response.
  • 使工人治其第，皆与己等 - They had workers build their homes, all equal to their own. This suggests a generous and equal treatment.
  • 祸之而益福之 - They cursed him but instead brought him more blessings. This is a paradoxical statement indicating a positive outcome despite initial negative intentions.
  • 深自愧悔 - They deeply regretted and felt ashamed. This expresses a strong sense of remorse.
  • 又仰其富，思交欢之 - They also admired his wealth and wanted to befriend him. This indicates a desire for social connection.
  • 因以贺仲阶进，备物而往 - So they sent a gift through their middleman, well-prepared. This shows a formal and thoughtful gesture.
  • 却之不得，系羊庭树 - He could not refuse, so he tied the sheep to a tree in the courtyard. This indicates a polite but firm acceptance.
  • 夜有僮被仆殴，忿趋树下，解羊索自经死 - At night, a servant was beaten by another servant, became angry, rushed to the tree, untied the sheep, and hanged himself. This describes a tragic event.
  • 其福之不如其祸之也 - His blessings were less than his curses. This is a paradoxical statement indicating a negative outcome despite initial positive intentions.
  • 后魏老，贫而作丐，仇每周以布粟而德报之 - Later, Wei became old, poor, and a beggar, and his enemies provided him with cloth and grain, which he gratefully accepted. This shows a change in circumstances and a final act of kindness.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 父既归 - The father returning home. This is a common scenario in traditional Chinese family life.
  • 兄弟不忍 - The brothers' emotional response. This reflects the importance of family ties and emotional bonds.
  • 析产而三之 - Dividing property. This is a traditional practice in Chinese inheritance laws.
  • 子得二，女得一也 - Gender-based distribution of inheritance. This reflects historical gender norms in Chinese society.
  • 大娘固辞 - The eldest sister's refusal. This shows respect for the eldest sibling's role in the family.
  • 吾等非姊，乌有今日 - Gratitude to the eldest sister. This emphasizes the importance of family support.
  • 福禄闻之皆流涕 - Emotional response to a story. This reflects the cultural value placed on storytelling and emotional connection.
  • 使工人治其第，皆与己等 - Building homes. This shows a commitment to equality and generosity.
  • 祸之而益福之 - Paradoxical outcome. This reflects the belief in karma and the unexpected consequences of actions.
  • 深自愧悔 - Regret and shame. This shows a deep sense of moral responsibility.
  • 又仰其富，思交欢之 - Admiration for wealth. This reflects the social value placed on material success.
  • 因以贺仲阶进，备物而往 - Sending gifts. This shows a formal and thoughtful gesture.
  • 却之不得，系羊庭树 - Accepting a gift. This shows a polite and firm acceptance.
  • 夜有僮被仆殴，忿趋树下，解羊索自经死 - Tragic event. This reflects the harsh realities of life and the consequences of violence.
  • 其福之不如其祸之也 - Negative outcome. This reflects the belief in the balance of good and bad fortune.
  • 后魏老，贫而作丐，仇每周以布粟而德报之 - Change in circumstances. This shows the cyclical nature of life and the importance of kindness.

Proper Nouns

  • 福禄 - The name of the person being described.
  • 魏 - The name of the person who later becomes a beggar.
  • 大娘 - The eldest sister.
  • 仲阶 - The middleman through whom gifts are sent.
  • 布粟 - Cloth and grain, items of basic necessity.
  • 仇 - The enemies of Wei, who show kindness to him in his old age.
